channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Something for the Weekend – 18/04/26
item.content:encoded:
It’s been a busy old week for me, and I’m glad for the weekend with sunny weather and the start of the World Endurance Championship at Imola. Six hours of roaring prototypes and GT3 cars is going to be great tomorrow.

But I mustn’t forget to play and write about some of the game’s I’ve seen recently – look out for Heave Ho 2, which I had a raucously good time playing at London Game Fest – and have on the go for review. Hopefully I find some more time for Pragmata too!

In the News This Week

There were some announcements during the Galaxies showcase, where we’ve picked a few to feature, as well as the reveal of an eagerly awaited sequel.

  • Metro 2039 revealed for PS5, Xbox Series & PC this winter
  • Peter Molyneux’s Masters of Albion launches into Early Access next week
  • Battlefield 6 roadmap promises classic maps, naval warfare, server browser & more
  • Sign up for the Hell Let Loose: Vietnam PC Playtest
  • Fortnite Save the World is now free to play, and on Switch 2 for the first time
  • STALKER 2 Sealed Truth update is live – explore the mysterious X-Lab in free new quest
  • Amazon Luna to remove all third party subscriptions and stores
  • TerraTech Legion rolls into the bullet heaven genre on 30th April

Games in Review & Featured Articles

Another very rich week for reviews, and while Pragmata is obviously the pick of the bunch, there’s plenty of other games that might draw your attention.

  • Pragmata – PS5, XSX|S, NSW2, PC – 9/10 (Video Review)
  • Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream – NSW2, NSW – 8/10
  • Mouse: P.I. for Hire – PS5, XSX|S, NSW, PC – 8/10
  • Dosa Divas – PS5, XSX|S, NSW2, NSW, PC – 8/10
  • Marathon (1 month review) – PS5, XSX|S, PC – 8/10
  • Starfield – PS5 – 7/10
  • Kotama and Academy Citadel – PC – 7/10
  • ICARUS Console Edition – PS5, XSX|S – 6/10
  • Sovereign Syndicate – PS5, XSX|S, PC – 6/10

Beyond that, Dom put a vibrating vest on once again to review the Woojer Vest 4, and augmented our main Pragmata review by taking a look at the Switch 2 release, which is a thoroughly enjoyable rendition of this game.

Aran revisited Hades II for the PS5 and Xbox Series X|S release, and absolutely loved his time in it, while the We Were Here Tomorrow’s PC demo gave me just enough of a taste to get me very interested.

Finishing up for the week, What We Played featured Pragmata, Dosa Divas & Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss.

Trailer Park

The 007: First Light title sequence features Lana Del Ray

Alan Wake drops into a Phasmophobia crossover event in May

Bandai Namco shared new Echoes of Aincrad gameplay

Northgard: Battleground revealed eight years after the original

Kitaria Fables 2 announced by PQube

Check out When Sirens Fall Silent Act 2

That’s the round up for this week. Hope you have a great weekend ahead and we’ll see you back here on Monday!



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/18/something-for-the-weekend-18-04-26/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite Save the World is now free to play, and on Switch 2 for the first time
item.content:encoded:
The original Fortnite Save the World game mode is now free to play, available across PS5, PS4, Xbox Series XS, Xbox One, Xbox Cloud Gaming, PC, GeForce Now, and Amazon Luna… and Nintendo Switch 2 for the first time. It is not, however, playable on smartphones, tablets, or the original Switch.

While Fortnite has morphed into a broad collection of games and experiences over the last few years, the Battle Royale mode remains the main meal for most players… but that’s not where Fortnite started. Before the Battle Royale came along to eat PUBG’s lunch, Fortnite was revealed and released as a paid co-op horde mode game, Save the World, where you’d team up with other players to build forts and fight back against monsters.

Save the World hasn’t really been a main focus for Epic for a long time – there was even a Save Save the World community campaign back in 2020 – but making it free to play gives it another chance to find a broader audience, and there’s now crossover Quests coming that will bridge Save the World and Battle Royale.

As part of making Save the World free for all, Epic has dished out some items for both existing owner, and for newcomers who pre-registered over the last few weeks.

These include for existing users:

  • 1x Hero Supercharger – Promote a Hero beyond Level 50.
  • 1x Weapon Supercharger – Promote a weapon beyond Level 50.
  • 1x Trap Supercharger – Promote a Trap beyond Level 50.
  • 5x Survivor Supercharger – Promote Survivors beyond Level 50.
  • 1x Hero Recruitment Voucher – Recruit a Hero of your choice.
  • 1x Weapon Research Voucher – Unlock a weapon of your choice.
  • 10000 Gold – Spend in the Event Store (to be renamed “the Exchange”) for valuable items.

And all players, old and new, who signed up for pre-registration earned the Kyle’s Construction Spray, a Save The World Jess Banner, and the Snowstrike Hero, which has the following perks:

  • Standard Perk: North Star
    • Throwing Stars will slow enemies for 4 seconds instead of dealing damage. Slowed-down enemies will explode for 167 base water damage if they receive melee damage or damage from another North Star explosion.
  • Commander Perk: North Star+
    • Throwing Stars will freeze enemies for 4 seconds instead of dealing damage. Frozen enemies will explode for 167 base water damage if they receive melee damage or damage from another North Star explosion.


https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/16/fortnite-save-the-world-is-now-free-to-play-and-on-switch-2-for-the-first-time/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
